name: 2019 ACR/EULAR Classification Criteria for IgG4-Related Disease
classification_rule: Three steps: (1) entry criterion met — clinical or radiologic involvement of a typical organ; (2) no exclusion criterion met (e.g. fever, ANCA, haemoptysis, positive ANA, CCP, certain biopsy findings, ~30 listed); (3) inclusion score ≥20 across 8 weighted domains. ALL THREE must hold to classify.

step_1_entry_criterion.label: Characteristic clinical or radiologic involvement of a typical organ (pancreas, salivary glands, lacrimal glands, retroperitoneum, kidneys, lungs, meninges, aorta, mediastinum, thyroid) OR pathologic evidence of a lymphoplasmacytic inflammatory process with IgG4+ cells

items.label: Dense lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate with obliterative phlebitis and/or storiform fibrosis
Dense lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate AND storiform fibrosis AND obliterative phlebitis (all three)
IgG4:IgG ratio 0.41 or higher, OR 10-50 IgG4+ cells/HPF
IgG4:IgG ratio ≥0.40 AND IgG4+ cells/HPF consistent with organ (tissue-specific cut-off)

todo: Encode the full 8-domain inclusion scorer + ~30-item exclusion checker — Day 2.

references.title: The 2019 American College of Rheumatology/European League Against Rheumatism classification criteria for IgG4-related disease
references.authors: Wallace ZS, Naden RP, Chari S, et al.
references.why_it_matters: First formal classification criteria for IgG4-RD. Three-step (entry → exclusions → scored inclusions) design prevents overdiagnosis by excluding mimics early. Validated sensitivity 82%, specificity 98%.

references.title: IgG4-related disease
references.authors: Stone JH, Zen Y, Deshpande V
references.why_it_matters: The definitive NEJM review that popularised IgG4-RD as a unified multi-organ entity. Explains the pathology triad (lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate, storiform fibrosis, obliterative phlebitis) that drives the scoring.

references.title: IgG4-related disease: an update on pathophysiology and implications for clinical care
references.why_it_matters: Contemporary Nature review covering T-follicular helper cells, CD4+ CTLs, plasmablast expansion, and rationale for B-cell depletion (rituximab) as first-line therapy.

references.title: Consensus statement on the pathology of IgG4-related disease
references.authors: Deshpande V, Zen Y, Chan JK, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Pathology consensus that defines the histologic triad used in the scoring system. Reference for reporting biopsies in suspected IgG4-RD.

references.title: International consensus guidance statement on the management and treatment of IgG4-related disease
references.authors: Khosroshahi A, Wallace ZS, Crowe JL, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Expert consensus on induction with glucocorticoids, use of rituximab in relapsing/steroid-intolerant patients, and monitoring serum IgG4 + imaging.
